skill.name: devflow
skill.description: Coordinate multi-agent software work from issue assessment through implementation, PR review, approval, and merge gates.
skill.summary: A structured workflow for agent coordination, requirement coverage, PR review, and merge readiness.
skill.author: Cygnusfear

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command references, plus weak-crypto and reconnaissance patterns. Review found no malicious code or prompt injection, but the skill does instruct agents to run GitHub and formatting commands and to perform PR operations, so it should publish with an operational-risk warning.

medium_findings.title: Operational External Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill instructs agents to use GitHub CLI commands and local formatting commands as part of PR workflow. This is legitimate for a development coordination skill, but it can affect repositories, branches, pull requests, and merge outcomes if used without repository permissions and human review.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced commands are explicit workflow instructions and match the skill purpose. Risk is operational rather than malicious because commands are documented, narrow, and mostly hardcoded with issue or PR placeholders.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis reported a weak cryptographic algorithm at the metadata description line. No cryptographic operation, secret handling, or encryption API appears at this location; the hit appears to come from text matching inside normal prose or metadata.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter text describing the skill. There is no evidence of DES, hashing, encryption, or any cryptographic implementation at this line.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis reported system reconnaissance around Mermaid diagram guidance. These lines describe documentation diagrams for reviews and PRs, not host inspection, environment probing, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are headings, prose, and Mermaid syntax examples. No evidence found of commands that inspect the host system or collect system information.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command References in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains command examples for GitHub issue and PR inspection, formatting, and issue-link verification. These commands are not hidden or obfuscated, but marketplace users should understand that following the skill can trigger repository and PR actions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command references are directly visible in Markdown code spans and shell blocks. They are consistent with the declared development workflow and do not show malware behavior.

content.value_statement: Complex agent development work can miss requirements, reviews, or issue links. This skill provides a phased workflow for assessment, parallel implementation, coverage checks, review, approval, and merge readiness.
actual_capabilities: Defines a five-phase workflow from issue assessment to merge continuation.
Requires full extraction and verification of original issue requirements.
Guides parallel agent implementation with branch and PR conventions.
Specifies a six-pass review process that starts with coverage verification.
Adds final merge gates for requirement coverage and issue linking.
Recommends Mermaid diagrams for flow, API, state, and architecture changes.
limitations: It is a process guide and does not provide executable automation scripts.
It assumes access to GitHub CLI, CI results, and repository permissions.
It focuses on GitHub issue and PR workflows rather than other trackers.
It still requires human approval for complex decisions and PR acceptance.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Parallel Feature Work
use_cases.description: Break a GitHub issue into agent tasks while preserving every requirement and dependency.

use_cases.title: Review Agent Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Run a structured review that checks coverage, runtime behavior, code patterns, abstractions, environment fit, and verification commands.

use_cases.title: Prepare Safe Merges
use_cases.description: Verify issue links, CI status, final requirement coverage, and merge order before completing a PR workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Assess an Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Use devflow to assess issue #123. Extract every requirement, identify dependencies, and propose parallel workstreams before implementation starts.

prompt_templates.title: Dispatch Agents
prompt_templates.prompt: Use devflow to create agent assignments for this issue. Include branch names, required PR links, verification commands, and clear coverage expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Pull Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use devflow to review PR #456 after CI passes. Start with requirement coverage, then complete the six review passes with evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Merge Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Use devflow to perform the final coverage gate for PR #456 and issue #123. Report missing requirements, issue-link status, and merge readiness.

output_examples.input: Assess issue #42 before starting agent work.
output_examples.output: A requirement list, dependency notes, proposed parallel tasks, and a coverage checklist for each assigned agent.

output_examples.input: Review a completed agent PR after CI passes.
output_examples.output: A pass-by-pass review summary with coverage status, blocking issues, verification evidence, and recommended next action.

output_examples.input: Check whether a PR can merge.
output_examples.output: A final readiness decision covering requirement completion, issue links, CI status, approval needs, and merge order.

best_practices: Start every workflow by extracting all original requirements from the issue or task.
Run the coverage gate before deeper review and again before merge.
Use human approval for significant tradeoffs, risky changes, or final PR acceptance.
anti_patterns: Do not merge when any original requirement is missing or only partially implemented.
Do not open or merge PRs without explicit issue links and verification evidence.
Do not use the workflow to bypass repository permissions, CI, or reviewer judgment.

faq.question: What does this skill help coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates multi-agent software development from issue assessment through implementation, review, approval, and merge checks.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It is a Markdown process guide, but it includes command examples that an agent or user may run.

faq.question: Why is full requirement coverage emphasized?
faq.answer: The workflow is designed to prevent partial implementations from passing review or reaching merge.

faq.question: Which tools does it assume?
faq.answer: It assumes a GitHub PR workflow, GitHub CLI access, CI results, and local formatting tools where relevant.

faq.question: Can it be used outside GitHub?
faq.answer: Parts of the process can transfer, but issue linking and PR checks are written for GitHub.

faq.question: What security warning applies?
faq.answer: The skill contains external command guidance and PR operations, so users should confirm permissions and human approval before acting.
